column20: professional and respctful and ready available for help
most of the time he leaves the unit and go to PICU to see the patients,sometimes he stays more than 30 minutes and this will delay the patients care and affect on the length of stay of the patients.

Dr. Kazzi does not respect his environment or the people around it. He is always on his phone talking in a loud voice on video call, he eats food with strong scents on the station which is very unprofessional. He does not manage patient flow and does not facilitate discharges especially when the ED is overloaded. He is always busy talking to someone so he does not follow up on patients properly and thus discharges or diagnoses are made late and patient satisfaction decreases.

Dr. Amin is a very knowledgeable physician and he defends the nurses in conflict which is something we are all grateful for. However, Dr. Amin is not available on shift, he is mostly on his phone or not even in the unit when we need him. He gets distracted and therefore does not manage patient flow effectively or efficiently. The unit usually becomes crowded with cases that can easily be seen and discharged quickly.
always respectful and professional. readily available to help his patients and ED staff
he is professional knowledgable and friendly
Very sweet and empathetic with everyone including healthcare workers. Working with him is easy and smooth
very respectful and always readily available for patients and staff. always professional.
he is very knowledgable and professional

Very sweet with both patients and staff
Very respectful
Loves educating both medical and non medical personnel
Shift goes smoothly with her even with a loaded ED
Dr Moukhaiber has been a great role model in dealing with a busy ED, managing parents' anxiety, and responding promptly to all their worries. Very grateful for her patience, teaching, and advice.
i haven't had a chance working with her
did not have a code over the last year

Dr. Kawthar always tries to solve patient’s problems concerning admissions or disposition or financial coverage, she always calls the ED admin on call for any problematic issue .
So I like to work with her since she solves the problems not only take medical decisions concerning patients.

Dr. Farah is a micromanager which makes me frustrated. He uses sarcasm and bullying to make fun of my input/concerns. His phone ringtone is always on loud and sounds like the arrest bell which startles me. He is a good MD in different areas but he is very stubborn and not receptive to feedback from nursing personnel.
Professionalism and respect in treating patients
i havent had a chance to work with him on the same duty
Dr Nagham is very respectful to patients, staff and colleagues and an excellent and very professional physician.
she is a hardworker , professional and very knowledgeable

Dr. Ghalayini is kind, respectful and it is always a pleasure to work with her on shifts. She has taught us many things and is always engaged in resident's education. She sees her patients in a timely fashion and always takes our input into consideration. 
She is a great asset to the department
very respectful and professional. always listens to patients concerns and is readily available to teach residents.
dr rachelle works very professionally. discusses each case and gives the suitable treatment.
She is kindhearted and listens to patients and answers nurses’ questions professionally. The duty goes by smoothly with her being an attending there. She is easy to talk to.
Dr El Zahran is professional with her peers, patients, and team. She maintains a professional relationship and efficient during shifts focused on improving patient flow and operational functions.

Iam satisfied with my performance
also my patients trust highly my decisions
very respectful and professional. always helping staff and patients.
